note: Mathlib/Dynamics/Ergodic/MeasurePreserving.lean:45 -- structure MeasurePreserving (f : α → β) (μa μb): Measurable f ∧ map f μa = μb; docstring 'f is a measure-preserving map w.r.t. measures μa and μb'. This IS the formalization of a measure-preserving transformation, the core object of a measure-preserving dynamical system (diagonal case α=β, μa=μb=μ gives the classical (X,μ,T) system).
verify_note: Decl confirmed at MeasurePreserving.lean:45 (namespace MeasureTheory open, name matches exactly); enwiki definition of (X,B,μ,T) requires T measurable + μ(T⁻¹A)=μ(A), which is literally MeasurePreserving T μ μ; Mathlib has no bundled DynamicalSystem structure, so the diagonal instantiation of this predicate is the standard unbundled formalization used throughout Mathlib/Dynamics/Ergodic -- exact is defensible.

note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/MeasurableSpace/Defs.lean:64 -- def MeasurableSet [MeasurableSpace α] (s : Set α) : Prop, 'MeasurableSet s means that s is measurable (in the ambient measure space on α)'. Mathlib has no dedicated probability-specific 'Event' wrapper; an event in a probability space is exactly a MeasurableSet of the sample space, so this is the general-measure-theory ancestor of the probability-theory notion, used that way throughout Mathlib/Probability.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at Defs.lean:64 (root namespace, name matches exactly); grepped Mathlib/Probability for a dedicated Event decl -- none exists, confirming the note's claim; Wikidata Q10290214 describes event as 'set of outcomes to which a probability is assigned', subclass of Set -- MeasurableSet is honestly the general ancestor, not overclaimed as exact, so match_kind=generalization is accurate.
